title: Building Homes for Bats: A Costly Project
summary: In Gloucestershire, England, a big road project is happening near the M5 motorway. As part of this, two special houses are being built for bats because the road work might disturb their homes. These bat houses cost £166,000, which is a lot of money. Some people, like councillors Julian Tooke and Vernon Smith, are worried about the high cost. They think the money could be used for other important things like schools or libraries. However, bats are protected by law, so the council has to find a new place for them to live. The council got four quotes to make sure they were paying a fair price. The new road junction should be finished by 2029.

background_read: Bats are small mammals that can fly and often live in groups called colonies. They are important for the environment because they eat insects and help pollinate plants. In many places, laws protect bats to keep them safe from harm. When big projects like building roads happen, sometimes animals like bats need to be moved to new homes to avoid disturbing them. This article talks about a road project in England where special houses are being built for bats, but some people are concerned about how much it costs.
Article_Structure: WHO: Councillors Julian Tooke and Vernon Smith, and the council in Gloucestershire
WHAT: Building two houses for bats as part of a road project
WHERE: Near junction 10 of the M5 motorway in Gloucestershire, England
WHY: Because bats are protected by law and need new homes due to the road work, but the high cost is causing concerns

perspectives.description: Some people think the £166,000 for bat houses is too much money and could be better spent on services like education or libraries.
The council has to build the bat houses because laws protect bats, and they got multiple quotes to ensure fair pricing.
